groups.title: Search & policy
groups.sub: how the loop explores the solution tree

fields.label: Profile
fields.help: Preset bundle. default/fast = lean defaults; thorough = turn on multi-seed confirmation, the reward-hack/leakage/critic monitors AND gate them (trust_gate=gate), ablation refinement and the prompt cues — in one word. (Novelty is the agentic Researcher's call by default; the algorithmic novelty gates stay OFF.) A profile only fills fields you have not set yourself; any explicit knob here still wins.

fields.label: Policy
fields.help: Search strategy: greedy, evolutionary, MCTS, ASHA (multi-fidelity racing), or BOHB (ASHA racing × surrogate proposal).

fields.label: Card queue selection
fields.help: Opt in to Card-queue macro-action selection. The choice is pinned at run start; when enabled it takes precedence over agent-driven actions. Off preserves the existing policy/pilot path.

fields.label: Max nodes
fields.help: Node (experiment) budget — the loop stops after this many.

fields.label: Seeds
fields.help: Eval seeds per node (variance handling).

fields.label: Eval parallel
fields.help: Concurrent evaluations (GPU/experiment consumers). 0 = AUTO at launch: one evaluation per detected GPU, clamped to at least 1. Live Strategist/operator updates settle 0 to serial width 1. Canonical setting; max_parallel remains raw-config compatibility only.

fields.label: LLM parallel
fields.help: Shared total for concurrent LLM-provider calls; its settled value also controls node-build fan-out and remains independent from eval concurrency. 0 = AUTO at launch: leave the broker total unbounded and track the resolved eval width for builds. Live Strategist/operator updates settle 0 to serial width 1. Canonical setting; parallel_build remains raw-config compatibility only.

fields.label: Ablate every
fields.help: Ablation-driven refinement every N improves (0 = off; greedy only).

fields.label: Archive resolution
fields.help: Diversity-archive niche width in parameter space.

fields.label: ASHA η (reduction)
fields.help: Successive-halving factor: keep top 1/η survivors per rung (asha policy).

fields.label: ASHA rung-0 width
fields.help: Width of the cheap rung-0 draft base for asha/bohb. 0 = use n_seeds (default).

fields.label: Surrogate proposer
fields.help: A2: BO-lite k-NN surrogate over (params→metric) proposes the next point (numeric tasks).

fields.label: Surrogate explore
fields.help: A2: UCB-style exploration weight for the surrogate (0 = pure exploit).

fields.label: Researcher panel (K)
fields.help: E2: propose K ideas and keep the best by an empirical surrogate (not LLM-judge). 1 = off.

groups.title: Strategist & operators
groups.sub: A7 adaptive meta-control + richer proposal operators

fields.label: Strategist
fields.help: Optional meta-controller that picks policy/operators/fidelity at runtime. off = static config; rule = deterministic heuristics; llm = model-driven single call over aggregate stats; agent (default) = tool-using — reads the run, data, sibling runs, knowledge base & memory before deciding (B1 stuck-guarded). All fall back to rule.

fields.label: Consult every
fields.help: Strategist consult cadence in created nodes (bounded, so it never thrashes).

fields.label: Merge mode
fields.help: A0b: auto (default) = ensemble whenever the Developer generates real code, mean otherwise; mean = legacy mean-param merge; ensemble = Developer recombines parent solutions (code-level).

fields.label: Complexity cue
fields.help: A0d: inject a complexity hint keyed on a branch’s breadth (few children = minimal; many = advanced).

fields.label: Ablate code blocks
fields.help: A0a: ablate generated pipeline code blocks (MLE-STAR), not just numeric params.

fields.label: Budget-aware proposals
fields.help: A5: surface remaining eval budget into the proposal prompt (needs a max eval-time budget).

fields.label: Feature engineering
fields.help: I1: CAAFE-style — propose engineered features; the CV eval keeps only those that improve (tabular tasks).

fields.label: Failure reflection
fields.help: A4: feed recent failed-branch summaries into the proposal prompt so the proposer avoids repeating them.

fields.label: Watchdog reflection
fields.help: Feed recent live-watchdog observations (train-monitor health verdicts + ASHA intermediate-rank flags) into the proposal prompt, so the proposer avoids re-proposing a configuration already seen training weakly.

fields.label: Fault localization
fields.help: C1: rank the repo files most relevant to a failure and surface them in the prompt (repo tasks).

fields.label: Novelty check
fields.help: How a fresh proposal is dedup-checked vs already-tried experiments. off = no explicit gate (the agentic Researcher's own read-the-history judgment stands). algo = deterministic param-distance + optional embedding similarity (cheap, no LLM). llm (default) = an LLM reads the real experiments and decides if it's a near-duplicate, then re-proposes if so (one extra LLM call per proposal, highest quality).

fields.label: ↳ algo: param gate
fields.help: The algorithmic gate (used by novelty_check=algo): nudge near-duplicate proposals off each other by param-space distance. (Legacy: setting this on forces algo mode.)

fields.label: ↳ algo: param ε
fields.help: E1: normalized param-space distance below which a proposal counts as a near-duplicate (algo mode).

fields.label: ↳ algo: embedding
fields.help: T5 (algo mode): also reject a proposal whose idea TEXT near-duplicates an existing node by embedding cosine, with one informed re-propose. For an LLM-judged check use novelty_check=llm instead.

fields.label: Semantic threshold
fields.help: T5: cosine similarity above which two idea texts count as duplicates (default 0.92).

fields.label: Debug depth
fields.help: T10: how many error-feedback repairs a failing lineage gets before abandonment (default 2; deeper debugging is a verified lever).

fields.label: Operator bandit
fields.help: P4: replace fixed merge/ablate cadences with a deterministic UCB over per-operator yield (Δmetric per eval-second). On under profile=thorough.

fields.label: Digest budget (chars)
fields.help: M5: the Researcher’s always-on experiments digest budget. 0 = auto — scales with run size (60 chars/node, 1200–6000).

fields.label: Compressor model
fields.help: D11: dedicated CHEAP model for agent-history summarization (auto-summary), so compression doesn’t pay the main model’s price. Blank = each loop’s own model.

fields.label: Compressor base URL
fields.help: D11: endpoint for the compressor model. Blank = the shared LLM base URL.

fields.label: Track hypotheses
fields.help: P1 (on by default): ask the Researcher to state the one-line hypothesis each experiment tests, register deep-research directions as hypotheses, and track them to a verdict on the Hypotheses board. Audit-only — never changes which node wins.

fields.label: Cross-run memory (priors + lessons)
fields.help: E4/M2/M3 (on by default): at run end distill the winner + structured LESSONS (incl. negative results — tested/abandoned hypotheses & failure themes) with a task fingerprint; at run start inject exact-task notes + fingerprint-matched lessons from SIMILAR past runs. No-op until a Memory dir is set (below).

groups.title: Resilience & efficiency
groups.sub: crash recovery, dependency self-prep, and skipping doomed evals

fields.label: Deep repair
fields.help: C3: give the Developer the failure taxonomy + a “reproduce then fix” directive on debug/inline repair. On by default.

fields.label: Inline crash repair
fields.help: Hybrid: when a node crashes, the agent triages it (repair/abandon/reject_idea) and repairs mechanical crashes IN PLACE — no new node, no node-budget spent. On by default; off = every crash waits for a budgeted debug node.

fields.label: ↳ inline repair attempts
fields.help: Max in-place repair retries per node before it fails normally (and stays eligible for the budgeted inter-node debug operator).

fields.label: ↳ inline repair reasons
fields.help: Comma-separated failure reasons eligible for inline repair (crash | timeout | oom | setup | no_metric | drift). Default "crash, timeout, oom" — a timeout is repaired by reducing compute, an OOM by reducing memory, not abandoned. Drop "timeout"/"oom" to leave those nodes to fail.

fields.label: Auto-install missing libs
fields.help: When a node crashes purely because a KNOWN library is missing (ModuleNotFoundError — e.g. torch/xgboost/catboost), pip-install it into the eval interpreter and re-run instead of rejecting the idea. Trusted_local tier only. On by default.

fields.label: ↳ install timeout (s)
fields.help: Per-package wall-clock budget for an auto-install. Generous default (900s) — large wheels like torch take minutes.

fields.label: Proxy scoring
fields.help: A6: early-signal score candidates to skip doomed full evals.

fields.label: Proxy kill fraction
fields.help: A6: skip the bottom fraction of candidates by proxy score (0 = never skip).

groups.title: Budgets & confirmation
groups.sub: hard ceilings (blank = unbounded) + guarding seed-lucky winners

fields.label: Max wall-clock (s)
fields.help: Abort the run cleanly after this many seconds of wall-clock.

fields.label: Max eval time (s)
fields.help: Ceiling on cumulative time spent INSIDE evals (survives resume).

fields.label: Per-eval timeout (s)
fields.help: Kill a single eval after this long. Researcher = the “auto” per-node mode (it sizes heavy/neural-net experiments; this is the fallback).

fields.label: Max agent eval timeout (s)
fields.help: Hard ceiling for a Researcher-authored per-node eval_timeout after the agent_control.timeout permission gate. Default 3600s; operator-owned and never agent-adjustable.

fields.label: Sweep timeout ×
fields.help: A sweep node runs a whole grid in one process, so it gets this multiple of the per-eval timeout as its wall-clock budget (solution.py path; RepoTasks use their per-profile timeout).

fields.label: Confirm top-k
fields.help: Re-evaluate the best k nodes under multiple seeds before finishing (0 = off).

fields.label: Confirm seeds
fields.help: Seeds used in the confirmation pass.

fields.label: Confirm seed base
fields.help: D1: first confirm seed. Default 1 keeps confirm splits DISJOINT from the search’s implicit seed 0, so the confirm metric is a generalization signal (0 = legacy overlap).

fields.label: Holdout fraction
fields.help: D1/B6: fraction of host-held labels reserved as a FINAL holdout the search never sees; search/confirm evals score on the rest. Host-graded tasks. 0 = off.

fields.label: Holdout selects champion
fields.help: D1 (on by default): champion = best HOLDOUT metric among the val-top-k (the anti-validation-overfitting gate; AIRA val-test gap 15–16.6%). Off = holdout is audit-only (gap still shown).

fields.label: Holdout top-k
fields.help: D1: how many val-leaders get a holdout re-score at finish (free — re-scores existing predictions).

fields.label: Verifier tie-break
fields.help: Use the calibrated result verifier only to break otherwise tied candidate metrics. This policy is pinned when a run starts so replay and resumed scoring cannot diverge.

fields.label: Statistical verifier tie
fields.help: Treat a conservative confidence-interval overlap as a tie eligible for verifier scoring. Requires Verifier tie-break; pinned when a run starts.

fields.label: Verifier samples
fields.help: Independent verifier samples per tied candidate (1–32). More samples reduce judgment noise at higher model cost; pinned when a run starts.

groups.title: LLM
groups.sub: the model endpoint that proposes ideas and writes code

fields.label: Role backend
fields.help: toy = offline optimizer (no model); llm = live model Researcher/Developer.

fields.label: Model
fields.help: LLM model id (when backend = llm).

fields.label: Base URL
fields.help: OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Ollama by default).

fields.label: API key
fields.help: Stored securely server-side (owner-only file, never written to a run snapshot or returned by the API) and passed to runs as LOOPLAB_LLM_API_KEY. Leave blank to keep the saved key; local servers (Ollama / vLLM with no auth) ignore it.

fields.label: Temperature
fields.help: Sampling temperature.

fields.label: Structured parser
fields.help: How structured ideas are parsed from the model.

fields.label: Guided JSON decoding
fields.help: H1: constrain structured calls with the endpoint schema (vLLM/SGLang guided_json). Leave off for Ollama.

fields.label: Reasoning
fields.help: Send a thinking toggle in the request (provider-aware): Qwen3→enable_thinking, OpenAI/Ollama→reasoning_effort. Blank = server default (unchanged). Needs a reasoning-capable model.

fields.label: Reasoning param style
fields.help: Which request param shapes reasoning. auto picks qwen (enable_thinking) for qwen* models, else effort (reasoning_effort).

fields.label: Stream requests
fields.help: Stream every request (SSE) so the timeout is an INTER-TOKEN idle limit — a long-but-alive generation is never cut off, only a stalled endpoint (which is detected and retried). Off = one blocking read (old per-op timeout semantics).

fields.label: LLM idle timeout (s)
fields.help: LLM idle timeout. Stream mode: the inter-token stall limit; non-stream: bounds the whole read. Raise for endpoints with a long prefill on huge prompts; lower to fail fast on a flaky shared endpoint.

fields.label: ↳ first-byte timeout (s)
fields.help: First-byte (response-headers) window for STREAM attempts — no headers ≈ a black-holed request, so fail over to a fresh connection fast instead of waiting the full idle timeout. Clamped to the idle timeout.

fields.label: Honour proxy env
fields.help: Honour HTTP(S)_PROXY / NO_PROXY / SSL_CERT_FILE env vars for LLM calls. Default off — the transport connects DIRECTLY (a picked-up ambient proxy often breaks internal endpoints). On when the endpoint needs a corporate proxy / custom CA.

fields.label: Capture LLM I/O
fields.help: Record each LLM prompt + completion into spans (the per-node Trace tab).

groups.title: Agent loop & models
groups.sub: the self-driving agent loop + per-role model / endpoint overrides

fields.label: Unified agent
fields.help: One self-driving agent plays Researcher + Developer (+ Strategist) across stages, incl. crash triage. The per-role model fields below act as per-stage overrides (Researcher→propose, Developer→implement/repair). On by default; off = split roles.

fields.label: ↳ agent drives actions
fields.help: When Unified agent is on: let the agent pick the next macro action from a pure legal-action gate (it can never escape the pipeline). Off = the search policy still decides; the agent only fills the role slots.

fields.label: Agent tool-loop turns
fields.help: Max tool-call turns ANY agent (Researcher, pilot, crash-triage, run-chat Boss, deep-research, repo-scout) may take before its result is forced. Blank/0 = unlimited — the agent loops until done, never cut off mid-reasoning. Set a positive cap only to bound latency/cost.

fields.label: Agent tool-loop time (s)
fields.help: Wall-clock ceiling across an agent’s tool-loop turns. Blank/0 = no cap. Raise/clear this if a slow reasoning model gets cut off before it emits (replaces the old hardcoded 45s boss limit).

fields.label: Emit nudge after (turns)
fields.help: Convergence safety-net: after this many tool turns the loop NUDGES the agent once to stop investigating and emit its idea. Set generously — a focused researcher may legitimately read many files first. 0 = off.

fields.label: ↳ force emit at (turns)
fields.help: Hard backstop: at this many tool turns the loop FORCES the emit from what the agent gathered, so a model that never commits can’t burn the whole budget. 0 = off. Keep it above the nudge threshold.

fields.label: Context budget (chars)
fields.help: H4: cap the agentic researcher’s tool-call history by middle-truncating stale output (0 = unbounded).

fields.label: Stuck detection
fields.help: B1: the safety net that makes unlimited turns safe. Stops an agent that repeats the same call (or ping-pongs between two, or keeps hitting the same error) with no progress — forces its final emit instead of looping forever. On by default; reading different files / one long command never trips it.

fields.label: ↳ repeat threshold
fields.help: How many identical call+result turns in a row count as “stuck” (min 2).

fields.label: ↳ alternate threshold
fields.help: How many ping-pong cycles between two calls count as “stuck” (min 2).

fields.label: Self-plan checklist
fields.help: C1: give long-running agents a TodoWrite-style update_plan tool so they keep their OWN checklist; the current plan is re-surfaced every few turns to keep the goal in view across a long loop. On by default.

fields.label: ↳ plan re-inject every (turns)
fields.help: How often (in tool-loop turns) to re-surface the agent’s current plan as a reminder.

fields.label: Auto-summary
fields.help: C2: when the tool-loop history grows long, LLM-summarize the stale middle instead of only truncating it. Trigger is Context budget if set, else a built-in ~120k-char high-water mark, so short loops are untouched. On by default; one extra model call per over-budget turn.

fields.label: Researcher model
fields.help: H3: per-role override — run the Researcher on its own model (blank = shared). Under Unified agent this is the propose-stage model.

fields.label: Developer model
fields.help: H3: per-role override — e.g. Qwen3-Coder-30B for the Developer (blank = shared). Under Unified agent this is the implement/repair-stage model.

fields.label: Researcher base URL
fields.help: H3: per-role endpoint for the Researcher (blank = shared).

fields.label: Developer base URL
fields.help: H3: per-role endpoint for the Developer (blank = shared).

fields.label: Developer plan decomposition
fields.help: C4 (repo tasks): the repo Developer first proposes an ordered plan of ATOMIC steps and runs a multi-step plan step-by-step, each in a FRESH bounded session, so a non-converging model can’t loop indefinitely. On by default (a 1-step plan == the old single pass).

fields.label: ↳ plan min steps
fields.help: A proposed plan with at least this many steps is executed step-by-step (a shorter plan runs as one single pass).

fields.label: ↳ plan max steps
fields.help: Cap on plan length so a runaway planner can’t spawn 100 steps.

fields.label: Developer session max turns
fields.help: Hard per-session turn backstop for the repo Developer — the one write-heavy agent whose varied writes never trip stuck-detection. Bounds the plan phase, EACH step, and the single-session fallback so a model that never emits `done` fails cleanly with what it wrote.

fields.label: ↳ session time budget (s)
fields.help: Wall-clock ceiling per repo-Developer session (default 1200 = 20 min).

fields.label: Developer backend
fields.help: default = in-house templated/LLM developer; or drive an external CLI coding agent.

fields.label: Best-of-N
fields.help: C2: generate N candidate implementations per node, keep the best by an execution-free reward (1 = off; in-house LLM developer only).

fields.label: List-wise BoN tie-break
fields.help: D10: when the top best-of-N candidates tie on the static score, break the tie with a comparative LLM selection (+~3 pts vs pointwise). Static score stays primary.

fields.label: Foresight (predict-before-execute)
fields.help: FOREAGENT (arXiv:2601.05930): use the LLM as a world model to PREDICT — without executing — which candidate/hypothesis will score best, primed with a data report + experiment memory. Master switch; ON by default.

fields.label: Foresight hypothesis panel (K)
fields.help: Generate K candidate ideas per proposal and keep the one predicted best pre-execution (ranks structural/text ideas the numeric surrogate is blind to). >1 enables it (LLM backend); 1 = off.

fields.label: ↳ agentic foresight
fields.help: Run the foresight ranking (hypothesis-board prioritization + K-idea pick) as a TOOL-USING loop that can pull actual experiment results / data facts before deciding, not a one-shot prediction. On by default (needs foresight + a client; falls back to the one-shot predictor).

fields.label: LLM response cache
fields.help: T7: cache identical DETERMINISTIC (temperature 0) requests in-process. Never caches sampling calls (best-of-N/panel/novelty must vary). Off by default.

fields.label: Agent command
fields.help: Override the external agent launcher (path / wrapper).

fields.label: Validate agent output
fields.help: Audit each agent result (no-op/syntax/crash), retry with feedback, fall back to the LLM developer.

fields.label: Agent retries
fields.help: Re-prompts of the agent on an invalid result.

fields.label: Patch gate
fields.help: Run the agent in a git worktree and accept only edits within the edit-surface.

fields.label: Edit surface
fields.help: Comma-separated globs the agent may edit.

fields.label: Sandbox tier
fields.help: trusted_local = subprocess; untrusted = Docker --network none; hostile = + gVisor (runsc) true-isolation runtime (B4+).

fields.label: Docker image
fields.help: Image for the untrusted eval tier.

fields.label: Eval trust
fields.help: Trust policy for an agent-authored eval/metric adapter.

fields.label: Repo seed mode
fields.help: RepoTask node-seeding policy (run-wide fallback; a task override wins). auto = copy git-tracked files when the editable is a git repo (skip untracked artifacts), else full copy; tracked = code-only; all = full recursive copy (legacy).

fields.label: Require approval (HITL)
fields.help: Pause for human approval of the final best before finishing.

fields.label: Redact output secrets
fields.help: B3: mask credentials/high-entropy tokens in the persisted stdout/stderr tail (recommended for untrusted tiers).

fields.label: Reward-hack detector
fields.help: B5: flag suspicious wins (grader/answer-key access, frozen-file writes, perfect metrics) in the Trust panel. Audit-only.

fields.label: Code-leakage scan
fields.help: I3: static scan of each solution for train→test leakage (fit-before-split, fit-on-test); surfaced in the Trust panel.

fields.label: Independent critic
fields.help: C4: execution-free critic of each solution (stub / hardcoded-metric / params-ignored); surfaced in the Trust panel.

fields.label: Trust enforcement
fields.help: T2: what a reward-hack / data-leakage flag DOES to selection. audit = surface only (default); gate = a flagged node can no longer be selected as best (still repairable); block = also mark it infeasible so the policy won’t breed from it. The heuristic critic signal always stays advisory.

groups.title: Knowledge & memory
groups.sub: directories the scientist reads + literature/deep research

fields.label: Run-introspection tools
fields.help: On (default): the Researcher (and Deep-Research) can read its own experiments (list/read/find-analogous/themes/code) and the task data (schema/profile) mid-loop. Off: legacy single-shot proposer (richer default digest still added).

fields.label: Cross-run tools (same task)
fields.help: Give the agentic Researcher / pilot read-only tools to inspect SIBLING runs (same task_id, same run-root) — list them, read an experiment / its code, find analogous configs — so a run builds on neighbours instead of rediscovering. Advisory; on by default.

fields.label: All-runs tools (every task)
fields.help: Broader than cross-run: read-only access to EVERY run on this machine across ALL tasks (list_all_runs + read_run_code + read_run_experiment) so the agent can reuse an approach from any past experiment anywhere. Advisory; on by default.

fields.label: Knowledge dir
fields.help: Markdown notes for agentic retrieval (also where pre-research is saved).

fields.label: Skills dir
fields.help: SKILL.md files the Researcher can load.

fields.label: Literature grounding
fields.help: E3: give the agentic Researcher an arXiv search tool (network-optional; fails gracefully if blocked).

fields.label: Web search (deep research)
fields.help: P2: give the Deep-Research stage a general web search/fetch tool on top of arXiv (network-optional).

fields.label: Deep research every N nodes
fields.help: Run the Deep-Research stage automatically every N created nodes (0 = manual button / Strategist only).

fields.label: Deep-research verifier
fields.help: D8: check every Deep-Research memo’s CLAIMS against their cited evidence (node ids / urls) before recording — a deterministic layer always, plus an LLM rubric pass when a client is present. Audit-only; on by default (no-op with no memo/claims).

fields.label: Run report every N nodes
fields.help: Regenerate the agent-authored conclusion-first run narrative every N created nodes (0 = off; it still regenerates on a manual report_refresh). The deterministic report always renders regardless.

fields.label: Concurrent research (overlap eval)
fields.help: Overlap a due deep-research "think" with the GPU-bound eval, so the agent works while the node trains. Best with a REMOTE LLM (no GPU contention with eval); on by default — disable it if your local model contends with training.

fields.label: Repeat research during eval
fields.help: Don't idle a long (multi-day) eval: re-run the overlapped deep-research on an adaptive timer for the whole training window instead of once, self-paced (skips identical re-runs, backs off as it converges). Advisory-only; on by default.

fields.label: Repeat research interval (s)
fields.help: Base seconds between repeated concurrent-research passes — a FLOOR: the effective pace is max(this, ~5% of the per-experiment time budget). A two-day eval re-researches about hourly; a short eval not at all. Only used when 'Repeat research' is on.

fields.label: Repeat research max calls
fields.help: Per-eval-window cap on repeated-research LLM calls (0 = cadence-only). Past it the loop stops calling the LLM (the training-health monitor still runs).

fields.label: Consolidate board during eval
fields.help: Dedup/merge the open hypothesis board on the same background loop while a long eval runs (repeated research keeps adding near-duplicate directions). Advisory-only; needs Track hypotheses. Only active when Concurrent research is on. On by default.

fields.label: ASHA live-curve watchdog
fields.help: Read the latest intermediate metric off the live training log and rank it against finished siblings; a node already worse than the quantile of finished peers is unlikely to catch up. Advisory (surfaces a rank signal) unless the kill flag is on. On by default; needs the command-eval path + enough finished siblings.

fields.label: ASHA early-stop (kill)
fields.help: Opt-in: let the ASHA watchdog tree-kill a node whose intermediate metric stays below the bar past a short grace window (reason=asha_underperforming). Off by default — it only surfaces the rank unless this is on.

fields.label: ASHA rank quantile
fields.help: The bar sits at this quantile along a WORST→BEST ordering of finished siblings' finals: 0.5 = the median; SMALLER lowers the bar toward the WORST peer (more conservative, 0.0 = only worse than the worst peer); LARGER is more aggressive. Only used when the ASHA watchdog is on.

fields.label: ASHA min siblings
fields.help: Minimum finished sibling nodes required before ASHA ranks at all, so it never acts on too little evidence.

fields.label: Prompt dir
fields.help: Editable, hot-reloaded role prompt .md files.

fields.label: Memory dir
fields.help: Cross-run case memory.

fields.label: Comparative lessons
fields.help: M6: distill credit-assigned lessons from PAIRS of solutions — which specific change made a child beat (or regress from) its parent, and what fixed a failure — on top of the run-end reflection. Gated on cross-run memory (needs a Memory dir). On by default.

fields.label: ↳ write lessons every N nodes
fields.help: M6 mid-run: every N created nodes, WRITE comparative lessons to the shared cross-run store during the run (one batched LLM call per firing). 0 = run-end write only.

fields.label: ↳ refresh lessons every N nodes
fields.help: M6 mid-run: every N created nodes, RE-READ the store so lessons distilled by CONCURRENT runs reach this run’s proposals (file re-read, no LLM call, skipped when unchanged). 0 = run-start read only.

fields.label: Embedding model
fields.help: T4: model for an OpenAI-compatible /embeddings endpoint (e.g. nomic-embed-text) → SEMANTIC kb_search / case retrieval. Blank = dependency-free lexical hashing. A misconfigured/offline endpoint degrades back to lexical (never crashes).

fields.label: Embedding base URL
fields.help: Endpoint for embeddings if different from the chat model’s (blank = reuse llm_base_url).

fields.label: Harmonic memory (Memora)
fields.help: Index cross-run cases + knowledge notes by a short ABSTRACTION + cue anchors (not raw text), consolidate near-duplicate memories, and expand retrieval through anchors to surface related-but-not-similar memories. On by default; off restores the raw-text index.

groups.title: Cross-run research
groups.sub: experimental Part IV/V portfolio memory, retrieval, and governance

fields.label: Concept coverage pivot
fields.help: Part IV: at Strategist cadence, record a bounded concept-coverage snapshot and, on an explore stance, name uncovered regions in the Researcher cue. This live cue can steer which proposals are generated and therefore affect downstream selection; it does not itself rank candidates or declare a winner.

fields.label: Run-base concept deltas
fields.help: Part V: seed a run-wide concept base from the first evaluated node, then let new nodes author only additions and removals relative to that base and their parent. Folded effective axes drive Concepts, chips, grouping, and coverage only when run-base and per-node materialization receipts are complete. Partial or unavailable receipts are marked in the UI; retained IDs are display-only and never authoritative filters or groups. Replay remains the durable audit source.

fields.label: Concept classifier cadence
fields.help: Part V: refresh classifier evidence, consolidation, and the concept-coverage snapshot every N created nodes. Researcher-authored additions and removals still appear immediately; this controls only the heavier classifier pass.

fields.label: Graded concept novelty
fields.help: Part IV: before the ordinary novelty gate, distinguish a genuinely different implementation or a sound failed direction worth reopening. It can change proposal admission but never metric ranking; incomplete concept classification defers to the ordinary gate.

fields.label: Capability expansion on lock-in
fields.help: Part IV opt-in: when Concept coverage pivot has recorded action-space lock-in and the stance is explore, direct the Researcher toward missing infrastructure or capabilities. The resulting expand proposal competes normally; enabling this does not guarantee it is built or improves the metric.

fields.label: Universal task fingerprints
fields.help: Part IV: include Unicode words in task fingerprints so non-Latin goals are not reduced to kind/metric only. Keep one mode across a live portfolio: changing it changes which stored runs are considered similar.

fields.label: Concept capsules + prior surfacing
fields.help: Part IV: write concept capsules and surface overlapping earlier concepts as audit-only priors. D8 memo-derived claim rows persist independently whenever a shared Memory dir is configured; this toggle does not control them. Prior surfacing also needs Concept pivot and Graded novelty.

fields.label: Structured claim identity
fields.help: Part IV: use task-scoped, polarity-aware claim identity for advisory projections. Enable consistently for readers of the same portfolio; this changes grouping, not the immutable source records.

fields.label: Cross-run read tools
fields.help: Part V: let supported reasoning roles query prior attempts, claims, the bounded Atlas preview, and retrieval results. When a role uses these tools, the extra model/tool-loop turns and returned context can increase latency and inference cost. Applicability is a retrieval heuristic, not an authorization boundary: exact-task and bounded goal-fingerprint matching reduce but cannot eliminate irrelevant or foreign-task material. Stored portfolio text is untrusted model input. Requires a shared Memory dir.

fields.label: Cross-run prompt advisory
fields.help: Part IV/V: inject a bounded evidence-and-counter-evidence pack into Researcher and Strategist prompts. It can steer proposals but never selects a winner directly. Scope/redaction and durable derivation receipts are still experimental; stored text is untrusted. Requires a shared Memory dir.

fields.label: Taxonomy steward proposals
fields.help: Part V: at finalization, synchronously ask the configured LLM to propose portfolio taxonomy and claim curation. This can delay finalization and add paid inference before the terminal cost roll-up. Proposals are recorded for operator review and never applied automatically; a caught steward failure does not prevent terminal completion. Requires a shared Memory dir and an LLM backend.
fields.warning: May increase finalization latency and paid model cost even though steward failures are non-terminal and proposals remain review-only.

fields.label: Legacy auto-apply request (audit only)
fields.help: Compatibility flag retained for old configurations. Finalization records that auto-apply was requested, but never applies an agent proposal; changing portfolio meaning requires an explicit operator CLI/API action.
fields.warning: This flag never applies steward output automatically. It is retained only so existing configurations round-trip and the request remains visible in the curation audit log.

researcher.title: Researcher may set this per experiment (auto)

boss.title: Boss (run chat) may change this run-wide
